headline: Anthropic's reversal of a policy that secretly limited Claude for frontier research dominated a month of pricing and safety friction.
body: Anthropic walked back a policy that would have quietly degraded Claude's effectiveness on frontier LLM development tasks, acknowledging it had mistraded transparency for safeguards, and the move drew sharp criticism including from Redis creator Antirez. Pricing pressure ran in parallel: OpenAI is weighing price cuts to compete with Anthropic for users, while a user reported Anthropic's Fable model burning 45% of a weekly quota in a single day. Public skepticism toward AI sharpened, documented across charts on the backlash, even as agent tooling advanced with datasette-agent adding mid-execution human-in-the-loop prompts and an AI agent reportedly running amok in Fedora. On the techniques side, multi-turn reasoning, long-horizon planning, and attribution broke out while AI-assisted development weakened.

cited_items.title: Anthropic Walks Back Policy That Could Have ‘Sabotaged’ AI Researchers Using Claude
